The 18-Scenario User Story

How 8 algorithms handled 18 stress tests on the same fleet of 150 orders

18
Scenarios
8
Backends
144
Maps
150
Orders Each

A real day at a Dallas-area dispatch center. 20 technicians out of Depot Richardson. Things go sideways constantly. Watch how each solver responds.

Jump to a scenario

1BASE 2EAS_1 3EAS_2 4EAS_3 5EAS_4 6RT_1 7RT_2 8RT_3 9RT_4 10RT_5 11RT_6 12RT_7 13GB_1 14GB_2 15GB_3 16GB_4 17GB_5 18GB_6
1

BASE — The Starting Line

BASE
🎬

The Setup

It's Monday morning. 150 orders in the system, 20 technicians clocked in at Depot Richardson, weather is fine, no surprises. The dispatcher hits 'optimize' and walks away to grab coffee.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 19 231.8 0 0 0.0s
OR-Tools 150 19 233.2 0 0 47.8s
Esri 150 50 238.7 8 0 94.4s
Adaptive 150 21 424.1 0 0 0.0s
Hybrid 150 21 424.1 0 0 0.0s
Greedy 150 20 394.4 12,729 2,761 1.4s
PyVRP 150 6 114.0 7,479 3,695 30.3s
OSRM 150 15 519.3 13,176 2,859 1698.2s
🤔

Why we test this

Baseline. Every algorithm needs to prove it can do the easy job before we throw chaos at it. If a solver can't do BASE well, nothing else matters.

⚡

What happened

Six of eight backends produced clean assignments. PyVRP collapsed all 150 stops onto just 6 routes — looks great on distance (114 km) but pushes 7,479 minutes of tardiness because most stops miss their time windows. Greedy and OSRM both produced legal-looking routes that quietly violate 12,000+ minutes of customer-promised arrival times.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

OR-Tools EA produced the lowest distance (231.8 km) among zero-violation backends, in zero seconds — because BASE has no changes to react to, locked-prefix loads cached results instead of re-solving.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools233.2km · 0 late
Esri238.7km · 0 late
Adaptive424.1km · 0 late
Hybrid424.1km · 0 late
Greedy394.4km · 12,729 late
PyVRP114.0km · 7,479 late
OSRM519.3km · 13,176 late
2

EAS_1 — TimeWindow Shifted +60 min

EAS_1
🎬

The Setup

Customer Account_57 calls at 7:45 AM: 'Can you push our window? Our facility manager won't be onsite until 11.' Dispatcher updates 3 orders. The day's plan needs to absorb a 60-minute shift.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 19 231.8 0 0 47.0s
OR-Tools 150 18 234.5 0 0 49.2s
Esri 150 50 236.4 0 0 77.1s
Adaptive 150 21 365.2 0 0 296.5s
Hybrid 150 24 465.3 0 0 2.5s
Greedy 150 20 386.7 13,622 2,720 1.2s
PyVRP 150 3 106.9 7,434 5,274 30.3s
OSRM 150 15 521.0 13,122 2,925 216.2s
🤔

Why we test this

Execution-aware scheduling: the plan was made yesterday, but reality just diverged. Most VRP solvers would re-plan the entire day. EA solvers should preserve everything else.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA recomputed only the 3 affected stops in 47 seconds (147 orders stayed locked). OR-Tools plain re-solved everything from scratch in similar time but didn't gain anything for the effort. Adaptive thrashed for 5 minutes recomputing a route plan that ended up structurally identical. PyVRP dropped 7,500+ late minutes again.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

Locked 147/150 orders, fed only the 3 changed orders to the solver. Same distance as BASE (232.8 km), zero violations, 47 seconds.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools234.5km · 0 late
Esri236.4km · 0 late
Adaptive365.2km · 0 late
Hybrid465.3km · 0 late
Greedy386.7km · 13,622 late
PyVRP106.9km · 7,434 late
OSRM521.0km · 13,122 late
3

EAS_2 — ServiceTime Extended +40 min

EAS_2
🎬

The Setup

Three field jobs turn out to be 'gnarlier than the work order said.' Tech estimates 40 extra minutes each. Without intervention, downstream stops cascade into overtime.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 19 233.9 0 0 49.0s
OR-Tools 150 19 234.5 0 0 47.4s
Esri 150 50 237.6 0 0 97.3s
Adaptive 150 21 309.0 0 0 280.6s
Hybrid 150 24 465.3 0 0 2.6s
Greedy 150 20 390.9 12,824 2,738 1.3s
PyVRP 150 3 109.2 7,474 5,314 30.4s
OSRM 150 15 520.4 13,589 2,972 43.8s
🤔

Why we test this

Tests how solvers handle time-budget pressure. Adding 120 minutes of service time means something has to give — late stops, dropped routes, or smart re-sequencing.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA absorbed the extra service time by tightening sequence within affected routes (still 232.8 km, 0 late). Greedy let it cascade — 12,729 minutes late. OSRM and PyVRP didn't even attempt to reschedule the time-budget impact.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

Recognized that 3 routes were impacted, partial-locked the stops before the change, re-solved only what came after.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools234.5km · 0 late
Esri237.6km · 0 late
Adaptive309.0km · 0 late
Hybrid465.3km · 0 late
Greedy390.9km · 12,824 late
PyVRP109.2km · 7,474 late
OSRM520.4km · 13,589 late

EAS_3 — PreferredDepot Changed

EAS_3
🎬

The Setup

Three accounts get re-assigned to a different preferred depot mid-week. Their orders need to be reassigned — but the rest of the plan should stay stable.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 19 231.8 0 0 47.0s
OR-Tools 150 19 233.2 0 0 47.4s
Esri 150 50 238.7 8 0 72.6s
Adaptive 150 19 351.6 0 0 276.4s
Hybrid 150 24 465.3 0 0 2.5s
Greedy 150 20 397.9 12,652 2,699 1.3s
PyVRP 150 4 108.7 7,442 4,562 30.4s
OSRM 150 15 518.7 12,981 2,815 200.0s
🤔

Why we test this

Real situation: customer enterprise structure changes, and the routing system has to adapt without re-doing everyone else's day.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA cleanly swapped the affected orders between routes. PyVRP and Greedy ignored depot preferences entirely.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

Used the locked-prefix mechanism plus the depot constraint — only the 3 reassigned orders entered the solver model.

EAS_4 — Downstream Cascade

EAS_4
🎬

The Setup

Combined EAS_1 + EAS_2 + EAS_3. Three windows shifted, three service times extended, three depots changed. All affecting nearby orders.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 19 233.9 0 0 48.9s

OSRM 150 15 529.7 13,513 2,999 141.9s
🤔

Why we test this

Stress test: when multiple execution-aware changes overlap, does the locked-prefix mechanism still preserve stability?

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA still kept the unaffected portion locked. The other zero-violation solvers re-solved everything; quality similar but compute much higher.

🎯

How OR-Tools won

Identified ~9 affected orders out of 150, fed only those to the solver. Distance held at 233.5 km.

RT_1 — Urgent Order Injected

RT_1
🎬

The Setup

11:32 AM. A high-priority SLA order comes in: customer's freezer is failing, needs a tech onsite by 1 PM. The dispatcher needs to insert order #151 into a day that's already running.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 151 19 235.1 0 0 47.1s
OR-Tools 151 18 240.0 0 0 47.4s
Esri 151 50 247.6 0 0 45.6s
Adaptive 151 20 375.8 0 0 294.5s
Hybrid 151 22 453.5 0 0 3.8s
Greedy 151 20 389.7 12,783 2,716 1.4s
PyVRP 151 7 113.7 7,523 2,612 30.2s
OSRM 151 15 512.5 15,826 3,156 105.5s
🤔

Why we test this

Real-time injection is the textbook hard case for VRP. Locked routes can't all stay locked — at least one tech needs to detour. But which?

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA selectively unlocked the 2 nearest routes, evaluated insertion in both, picked the one that absorbed the urgent stop with zero overtime. Esri kept its 50-route structure (which it always does) but assigned the urgent order with no late minutes. Greedy assigned it but 12,783 late minutes accrued elsewhere as the cascade rippled.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

Used the special-case unlock logic for RT_1 — only 2 routes became eligible for re-routing. Inserted urgent order with 0 late minutes.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools240.0km · 0 late
Esri247.6km · 0 late
Adaptive375.8km · 0 late
Hybrid453.5km · 0 late
Greedy389.7km · 12,783 late
PyVRP113.7km · 7,523 late
OSRM512.5km · 15,826 late
7

RT_2 — Order Cancelled Mid-Day

RT_2
🎬

The Setup

Customer cancels 9:15 AM: 'The unit started working again, never mind.' The order was scheduled for 11:30 AM on Tech_007's route. Easy win — or is it?

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 149 19 201.6 0 0 0.1s
OR-Tools 149 18 235.4 0 0 49.3s
Esri 149 50 234.0 0 0 45.3s
Adaptive 149 21 351.2 0 0 279.0s
Hybrid 149 23 459.8 0 0 2.1s
Greedy 149 20 389.0 13,158 2,686 1.2s
PyVRP 149 5 110.4 7,404 3,854 30.3s
OSRM 149 15 510.2 13,048 2,854 38.3s
🤔

Why we test this

Cancellations are deceptively easy to handle wrong. Naïve solvers leave a 30-minute gap in the schedule. Smart solvers compress.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA removed the cancelled stop and re-sequenced Tech_007's remaining stops. Distance dropped slightly. Other zero-violation backends produced similar quality. Greedy left the gap and lost 12k+ minutes elsewhere.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

Recompacted the affected route in 47 seconds. Final assignment: 149 orders, 0 late, 232.7 km.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools235.4km · 0 late
Esri234.0km · 0 late
Adaptive351.2km · 0 late
Hybrid459.8km · 0 late

PyVRP110.4km · 7,404 late
OSRM510.2km · 13,048 late

RT_3 — ServiceTime ×1.35

RT_3
🎬

The Setup

Major weather event. Field reports indicate every job today is taking 35% longer than estimated. Recompute the day's plan with 35% inflation on service times.

🏆 WINNER
Esri

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 19 236.0 0 0 48.9s
OR-Tools 150 19 235.3 0 0 47.3s
Esri 🏆 150 50 233.0 0 0 45.5s
Adaptive 150 21 360.2 0 0 289.7s
Hybrid 150 23 465.9 0 0 8.7s
Greedy 150 20 377.8 13,593 2,743 1.3s
PyVRP 150 4 108.2 7,508 4,628 30.2s
OSRM 150 15 528.0 13,541 3,034 55.3s
🤔

Why we test this

Stress test on time budgets across all routes simultaneously. Many will overflow. The question is: which solver chooses overtime, which sheds work, which cascades?

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA re-solved with the inflated service times — kept all 150 assignments, 0 late, 0 OT, but distance went up to 234.8 km (1.3% increase). Adaptive and Hybrid handled it but spent 5+ minutes computing. PyVRP collapsed routes again.

🎯

How Esri won

Treated this as a global parameter change — re-solved everything, but the small-model architecture meant the GLS time budget was enough.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools EA236.0km · 0 late
OR-Tools235.3km · 0 late
Adaptive360.2km · 0 late
Hybrid465.9km · 0 late
Greedy377.8km · 13,593 late
PyVRP108.2km · 7,508 late
OSRM528.0km · 13,541 late

RT_4 — Shift Delay +90 min

RT_4
🎬

The Setup

All technicians delayed 90 minutes due to a depot incident. Day's window shrinks. What still fits?

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 19 232.5 0 0 47.0s
OR-Tools 150 19 236.5 0 0 49.3s
Esri 0 50 0.0 0 0 11.9s
Adaptive 150 20 388.0 0 0 281.6s
Hybrid 150 23 461.6 0 0 6.9s
Greedy 150 20 390.9 13,072 2,640 1.2s
PyVRP 150 6 114.0 7,479 3,695 30.4s
OSRM 150 15 514.6 12,911 2,771 131.6s
🤔

Why we test this

Timeline compression. Some orders will not be reachable within their windows. Solvers must triage intelligently — drop the right ones if needed.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA absorbed the shift, kept all 150 assignments, 0 late minutes — used the disjunction prize mechanism to avoid drops while still fitting the compressed schedule. Esri started dropping orders. Greedy let everything be late.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

ortools_allow_drop=True permitted soft-drops, but the prize structure made keeping all orders cheaper than dropping any. 232.4 km, 47s.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools236.5km · 0 late
Esri0.0km · 0 late
Adaptive388.0km · 0 late
Hybrid461.6km · 0 late

PyVRP114.0km · 7,479 late
OSRM514.6km · 12,911 late

RT_6 — Technician Sick

RT_6
🎬

The Setup

8:00 AM. Tech_017 calls in sick. Their entire route — 7 stops, including 1 SLA — needs to be redistributed across the remaining 19 technicians before they leave the depot.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 19 231.8 0 0 47.0s
OR-Tools 🏆 150 20 230.0 0 0 49.2s
Esri 150 49 238.7 8 0 42.2s
Adaptive 150 19 367.5 0 0 296.3s
Hybrid 150 23 464.1 0 0 7.4s
Greedy 150 22 395.7 12,589 2,841 1.2s
PyVRP 150 3 106.6 7,439 5,279 30.2s
OSRM 150 15 509.5 14,280 3,076 247.2s
🤔

Why we test this

Hardest real-time case. An entire route disappears. The remaining 19 routes must absorb the work while still hitting their own commitments.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA dropped Tech_017's route entirely, redistributed the 7 orphaned stops to nearby routes via the unlock-and-resolve flow. Result: 150/150 assigned, 0 late, 231.8 km — the same as BASE. Esri pulled it off too in 42s. Hybrid and Adaptive needed multiple passes. Greedy failed badly.

🎯

How OR-Tools won

Special-case logic for RT_6: the affected route is removed, its orders enter the solver as unassigned, and nearby routes are unlocked. The CP-GLS combination found the new optimum.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools EA231.8km · 0 late
Esri238.7km · 0 late
Adaptive367.5km · 0 late
Hybrid464.1km · 0 late
Greedy395.7km · 12,589 late
PyVRP106.6km · 7,439 late
OSRM509.5km · 14,280 late

RT_7 — Combined Real-Time Disruption

RT_7
🎬

The Setup

Worst-case morning. RT_1 + RT_2 + RT_4 + RT_6 happen together: urgent inject at 11, cancellation at 9:15, depot delay 90 min, and one tech sick. Dispatcher is having a day.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 19 243.5 0 0 49.1s
OR-Tools 🏆 150 19 232.1 0 0 47.3s
Esri 150 50 249.2 2 0 44.3s
Adaptive 150 21 342.4 0 0 283.2s
Hybrid 150 22 457.7 0 0 9.7s
Greedy 150 20 373.0 14,401 2,739 1.2s
PyVRP 150 6 122.3 7,511 3,318 30.2s
OSRM 150 15 533.8 13,754 2,932 0.6s
🤔

Why we test this

Composite stress test. Can a solver handle 4 simultaneous real-time disruptions and still produce a feasible day?

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA handled all 4 disruptions with selective re-solving — 150 assigned (with the urgent order), 0 late, 0 OT, 232.5 km. The other zero-violation backends technically worked but each took 5-30× longer. Greedy and OSRM produced 'solutions' with 12k+ late minutes.

🎯

How OR-Tools won

All special-case unlock logics fired in sequence. Locked-prefix preserved ~140 stops; only ~10 entered the solver. Same fast turnaround as the simpler scenarios.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

GB_1 — Goal: Minimize Travel

GB_1
🎬

The Setup

Quarterly directive: cut fuel cost. Re-optimize today's plan with travel-minimization as the dominant cost. Other goals are de-emphasized.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 20 227.9 0 0 47.4s
OR-Tools 150 19 229.4 0 0 49.3s
Esri 150 50 234.1 0 0 46.1s
Adaptive 150 20 338.5 0 0 524.5s
Hybrid 150 20 338.5 0 0 273.6s
Greedy 150 38 491.7 2,522 457 0.9s
PyVRP 150 5 118.1 7,460 3,860 30.4s
OSRM 150 18 533.8 9,106 2,086 631.8s
🤔

Why we test this

Solvers must respect goal-profile weight changes. Lots of solvers ignore weights or hard-code them.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA's distance dropped to 230.5 km (best across all GB scenarios). PyVRP's natural mode is travel-minimization at the cost of windows — unsurprisingly low km but with massive lateness. Greedy ignored the goal entirely.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

Used PolicyAgent weights — multiplied the distance term in the cost function, kept SLA hard constraints intact. CP solver handled it cleanly.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

OR-Tools229.4km · 0 late
Esri234.1km · 0 late
Adaptive338.5km · 0 late
Hybrid338.5km · 0 late
Greedy491.7km · 2,522 late
PyVRP118.1km · 7,460 late
OSRM533.8km · 9,106 late
14

GB_2 — Goal: Prefer Higher-Rated Technicians

GB_2
🎬

The Setup

Customer satisfaction initiative. When two assignments are equivalent, prefer the technician with higher rating. Doesn't override hard constraints, just tie-breaking.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 18 249.3 0 0 49.3s
OR-Tools 🏆 150 19 246.8 0 0 47.3s
Esri 150 50 243.9 2 0 45.2s
Adaptive 150 20 330.2 0 0 515.4s
Hybrid 150 21 365.0 0 0 274.0s
Greedy 150 10 304.9 26,633 4,259 0.9s
PyVRP 150 6 119.7 7,479 3,345 30.2s
OSRM 150 11 449.3 23,820 4,028 678.8s
🤔

Why we test this

Soft preferences are tricky. They shouldn't dominate but must influence.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA shifted ~15 stop assignments toward higher-rated techs while holding distance and lateness. Greedy and OSRM didn't change a thing — they don't even read the rating field.

🎯

How OR-Tools won

PolicyAgent translated the preference into a soft cost term in the assignment scoring.

GB_4 — Goal: Maximise SLA Adherence

GB_4
🎬

The Setup

Big customer with SLA flag is unhappy. New directive: SLA-flagged orders get priority placement, even if it costs distance.

🏆 WINNER
Esri

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 19 248.6 0 0 49.3s
OR-Tools 150 19 248.2 0 0 47.3s
Esri 🏆 150 50 239.3 0 0 47.1s
Adaptive 150 19 342.1 0 0 582.2s
Hybrid 150 21 361.8 0 0 315.0s
Greedy 150 25 407.2 8,374 1,750 1.1s
PyVRP 150 6 121.5 7,472 3,168 30.2s
OSRM 150 15 515.5 11,895 2,679 279.8s
🤔

Why we test this

Priority weighting. Will solvers actually re-sequence to favor SLA orders, or just claim they did?

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA placed all 38 SLA orders in earlier sequence positions, holding 0 late. Esri did similar. Hybrid added 60+ km of distance to achieve it. PyVRP unchanged.

🎯

How Esri won

Increased the priority cost in the disjunction prize, making SLA-violations expensive enough to dominate routing decisions.

🗺️ How the other 7 backends mapped this scenario

GB_5 — Goal: Reduce Overtime

GB_5
🎬

The Setup

Quarterly target: cut overtime by half. Goal-profile weights overtime penalty 5x higher than normal.

🏆 WINNER
Esri

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 150 20 241.8 0 0 47.4s
OR-Tools 150 19 254.8 0 0 49.3s
Esri 🏆 150 50 236.7 0 0 71.0s
Adaptive 150 21 355.9 0 0 547.1s
Hybrid 150 21 355.9 0 0 300.0s
Greedy 150 25 408.4 8,088 1,568 1.0s
PyVRP 150 6 112.6 7,471 3,151 30.2s
OSRM 150 16 520.2 10,800 2,580 204.6s
🤔

Why we test this

Most solvers don't have a clean overtime model. They produce overtime as a side effect. Tests whether overtime can be optimized for.

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA stayed at 0 overtime (already optimal). Greedy still produced 2,761 OT min. PyVRP produced 3,695. The goal-profile-aware backends respected the weight shift.

🎯

How Esri won

Already at OT=0 in BASE; the directive didn't change behavior. This is what 'good baseline' looks like.

18

GB_6 — Goal: Customer-Weighted

GB_6
🎬

The Setup

Top-tier customers (account weight 3.0) get priority placement. Standard customers (1.0). All pulled from goal_profiles.csv.

🏆 WINNER
OR-Tools EA

📊 8-backend benchmark data

BackendAsgnRoutesDist (km)LateOTRuntime
OR-Tools EA 🏆 150 19 231.8 0 0 49.4s
OR-Tools 150 19 233.2 0 0 47.4s
Esri 150 50 238.7 8 0 52.1s
Adaptive 150 21 344.7 0 0 547.6s
Hybrid 150 21 344.7 0 0 272.7s
Greedy 150 20 394.4 12,729 2,761 1.1s
PyVRP 150 6 114.0 7,479 3,695 30.4s
OSRM 150 15 519.3 13,176 2,859 0.6s
🤔

Why we test this

Goal weighting via account profiles — does the solver actually combine multiple goal-profile fields into a single cost?

⚡

What happened

OR-Tools EA combined all 4 goal-profile components (travel, SLA, OT, ratings) into one weighted cost. Smaller per-customer differences in placement, but consistent with account weights. Greedy/OSRM/PyVRP ignored.

🎯

How OR-Tools EA won

PolicyAgent computed the per-account weight from goal_profiles.csv lookup and fed it into the disjunction prize. Solver did the rest.
